learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Loops, XOR Operator, Arithmetic Series, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine assigning roll numbers from 0 to n to students. One student is absent, so one roll number is missing from the attendance list. Your task is to identify the missing roll number.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine assigning roll numbers from 0 to n to students. One student is absent, so one roll number is missing from the attendance list. Your task is to identify the missing roll number.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use the XOR pattern whenever one number is missing from a complete range of unique integers.
learning.signals: Whenever exactly one unique number is missing from a complete range, think of XOR or the arithmetic sum formula.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing from the array.

examples.explanation: The numbers 0,1,2,3 should exist. The missing number is 2.
The range is [0,2]. The missing number is 2.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Check every number from 0 to n and search whether it exists in the array.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: The numbers should form a complete sequence from 0 to n. Either compute the expected sum and subtract the actual sum, or use XOR since identical numbers cancel each other.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Every matching number cancels itself using XOR.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever exactly one unique number is missing from a complete range, think of XOR or the arithmetic sum formula.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Every number appearing in both the index range and the array cancels out because a XOR a = 0. Only the missing number remains.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever exactly one unique number is missing from a complete range, think of XOR or the arithmetic sum formula.
commonWrongPaths: Initializing XOR with 0 instead of n.
Overflow concerns with the sum formula in other languages.

commonMisconceptions: Initializing XOR with 0 instead of n.
Overflow concerns with the sum formula in other languages.

pattern.name: XOR / Mathematical Formula
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever exactly one unique number is missing from a complete range, think of XOR or the arithmetic sum formula.

approaches.title: Linear Search
approaches.idea: Check every number from 0 to n and search whether it exists in the array.
algorithm: Search the number in the array.

code.java: Nested search omitted because of quadratic complexity.

approaches.title: Mathematical Formula
approaches.idea: Compute the expected sum of numbers from 0 to n using the arithmetic series formula, then subtract the actual array sum.
approaches.dryRun: Expected sum - Actual sum = Missing number.

approaches.title: XOR
approaches.idea: XOR all indices from 0 to n together with all array elements. Since identical numbers cancel each other, the remaining value is the missing number.
approaches.intuition: Every number appearing in both the index range and the array cancels out because a XOR a = 0. Only the missing number remains.

visualization.description: Matching numbers cancel each other using XOR.

interviewFollowUps: Which is better: Sum Formula or XOR?
How would you find two missing numbers?
interviewTips: Explain why XOR cancels duplicate values.
State O(n) time and O(1) space.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Every matching number cancels itself using XOR.

commonMistakes: Initializing XOR with 0 instead of n.
Overflow concerns with the sum formula in other languages.

relatedProblems: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
